tema: B1 Grand Review — All Topics Together

banco_oraciones.oracion: Abuela Carmen used to sell mangoes at the market every morning.
In 1982, she sold mangoes to Gabriel García Márquez himself.
She has never forgotten his kind smile.
Carmen has been living in Medellín since 1975, the year she won a cumbia contest.
If she could travel back in time, she would dance at that contest again.
The winning dance was passed down to Sofía, quietly, over the years.
Sofía has always loved to dance, but she rarely admits it in public.
Next year, the family is going to celebrate Carmen's story with a big party.

interaccion: Carmen: Sit down, everyone. I want to tell you something I've never told you before.
Carmen: In 1982, I **sold** mangoes to a famous writer. Gabriel García Márquez, in person!
Kenji (on video call): Carmen, you are full of surprises.
inicio.definicion_pragmatica: This is everything B1 gave you, working together: real conditions, finished and unfinished pasts, passive facts and future plans — one level, one story.

filas: If I had time, I would travel more
She has already run a marathon
He has been studying for six months
I've been to Madrid. I went in 2020
I'll help you! / I'm going to travel in June
She used to live in the countryside
I enjoy reading / I want to read

ejercicios.pregunta: In 1982, she ___ mangoes to García Márquez.
Carmen ___ live in the countryside before Medellín.
If she ___ time, she would travel more.

ejercicios.afirmacion: 'Coffee is grown in Colombia' is passive voice.
We use 'going to' for a decision made at the moment of speaking.

ejercicios.texto: Sofía has always loved ___ (dance), but she rarely admits it.
She danced so ___ (careful) that nobody noticed her mistake.

ejercicios.respuesta: She has never forgotten his kind smile
The winning dance was passed down

ejercicios.texto_con_error: She didn't used to dance in public.
ejercicios.respuesta: She didn't use to dance in public.

ejercicios.texto_con_error: Look at those clouds — it will rain!
ejercicios.respuesta: Look at those clouds — it's going to rain!

ejercicios.oracion_base: She doesn't have time, so she doesn't travel more.
ejercicios.respuesta: If she had time, she would travel more.

ejercicios.oracion_base: They passed the winning dance down to Sofía.
ejercicios.respuesta: The winning dance was passed down to Sofía.

ejercicios.oracion_base: Carmen used to live in the countryside.
ejercicios.respuesta: Carmen didn't use to live in the countryside.

ejercicios.pregunta: What is something you have never forgotten?
ejercicios.respuesta: (respuesta libre, ej. I have never forgotten my first day of school.)

ejercicios.pregunta: What do you enjoy doing carefully, and why?
ejercicios.respuesta: (respuesta libre, ej. I enjoy cooking carefully because I love good food.)

guion: Carmen: Sit down, everyone. I want to tell you something I've never told you before.
Carmen: In 1982, I sold mangoes to a famous writer. Gabriel García Márquez, in person!
Ben: No way! What did he say?
Carmen: He said my mangoes were the sweetest in Colombia. I've been proud of that for over forty years.
Sofía: And is it true you won a cumbia contest?
Carmen: In 1975! If I had the energy, I would dance right now.
Ben: Please don't. My back still hurts from the last party.

comprension.pregunta: Who did Carmen sell mangoes to in 1982?

reading.texto: Abuela Carmen has lived many lives inside one life. She has been living in Medellín since 1975, the year she won a cumbia contest that people still talk about. Before that, she used to sell mangoes at the market every morning, and once, in 1982, she sold a bag of mangoes to Gabriel García Márquez himself. She has never forgotten his kind smile. If she could go back, she says she would dance at that contest again — carefully, but without fear. Next year, the family is going to celebrate her story with a big party, and this time, everyone is going to dance.

preguntas.afirmacion: Carmen has been living in Medellín since 1975.
Carmen sold mangoes to a famous singer.
The family's party has already happened.
